learnings.pattern: CRITICAL: When fine-tuning a causal LM (GPT-2, DistilGPT-2) for CLASSIFICATION tasks (sentiment, NLI, etc.), you MUST use AutoModelForSequenceClassification, NOT AutoModelForCausalLM. Causal LM predicts next tokens and will give 0.0 accuracy on classification. Always match model class to task type.
learnings.context: Any task involving classification (sentiment analysis, topic classification, NLI) with decoder-only models like GPT-2

learnings.pattern: GPT-2 requires explicit pad_token_id configuration when using MPS/GPU, otherwise batch_size > 1 causes runtime errors
learnings.context: When fine-tuning GPT-2 on Apple MPS or CUDA with batch sizes greater than 1

learnings.pattern: The peft library provides reliable LoRA implementation with minimal boilerplate compared to manual implementation
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA for transformer models like GPT-2

learnings.pattern: 3 epochs with full SST-2 training data (2K+ samples) and multiple seeds provides statistically meaningful accuracy comparisons
learnings.context: When benchmarking LoRA vs full fine-tuning on text classification

learnings.pattern: Creating a streamlined run_metrics.py script is more reliable than complex experiment runners for initial validation
learnings.context: When testing training scripts before running full experiments

learnings.pattern: Using too few training samples leads to unreliable metrics; full SST-2 dataset (2K+ samples) was required for valid experiments
learnings.context: When measuring model accuracy differences between fine-tuning approaches

learnings.pattern: Large batch size (4) with gradient accumulation (4) combined with long sequence length (128) causes memory/timeout issues on Apple Silicon MPS, requiring reduction to batch_size=1 and seq_len=64
learnings.context: When running LoRA fine-tuning on DistilGPT-2 with MPS backend

learnings.pattern: Loading models directly to MPS can cause 224s+ timeouts; need to load to CPU first then move to MPS or use CPU device initially
learnings.context: When initializing DistilGPT-2 on Apple Silicon devices

learnings.pattern: HuggingFace PEFT library provides reliable LoRA implementation compared to custom implementations, reducing debugging overhead
learnings.context: For parameter-efficient fine-tuning tasks

learnings.pattern: Generating synthetic/perplexity data without running actual training leads to invalid benchmarks; must execute training runs before creating comparison plots
learnings.context: When creating evaluation visualizations

learnings.pattern: DistilGPT-2 with LoRA requires conservative hyperparameters: small batch sizes, shorter sequences, and reduced gradient accumulation steps to fit in memory-constrained environments
learnings.context: When fine-tuning on consumer hardware with limited VRAM

learnings.pattern: Using HuggingFace PEFT library for LoRA implementation on DistilGPT-2 is straightforward and functional - the training pipeline completed successfully with r=16, α=32
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA PEFT on small transformer models like DistilGPT-2

learnings.pattern: Evaluation and visualization tasks failed while training succeeded, indicating evaluation code had issues (possibly dataset loading, memory, or code bugs) separate from the training pipeline
learnings.context: When running post-training evaluation tasks on SST-2 dataset

learnings.pattern: Full dataset evaluation (67k samples) may cause memory or runtime issues - smaller sample evaluations worked while full dataset runs failed
learnings.context: When evaluating on SST-2 benchmark with large sample counts

learnings.pattern: LoRA hyperparameters r=16 (rank) and α=32 (scaling) worked well for DistilGPT-2 text classification
learnings.context: When fine-tuning DistilGPT-2 with LoRA for classification tasks

learnings.pattern: Switch from Causal Language Modeling to Sequence Classification head for sentiment classification tasks like SST-2 to reduce training time and improve efficiency.
learnings.context: When fine-tuning for classification tasks, using CLS head instead of causal LM is faster and more appropriate

learnings.pattern: Starting with too small dataset (20 samples) resulted in poor accuracy (62%), requiring subsequent increase to 2K-5K samples to reach 90-95% accuracy.
learnings.context: Dataset size below 1K samples is insufficient for meaningful fine-tuning results on sentiment classification

learnings.pattern: Reduce epochs and switch model head to prevent timeout on resource-constrained hardware like Apple M3 Max MPS.
learnings.context: When training on consumer hardware with time constraints, start with 1-2 epochs and adjust as needed

learnings.pattern: LoRA with r=16 rank provided effective parameter-efficient fine-tuning for DistilGPT-2 on downstream classification.
learnings.context: Rank 16 offers good balance between efficiency and performance for small-to-medium language models

learnings.pattern: HuggingFace PEFT library provides reliable LoRA implementation with clean abstractions for parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
learnings.context: PEFT library abstracts LoRA config complexity and integrates well with Transformers and Trainer

learnings.pattern: SST-2 test set has no labels (all zeros). Always use the validation set for evaluation, not the test set.
learnings.context: When fine-tuning on SST-2, the test set is unlabeled so evaluation fails. Must load validation set for metrics.

learnings.pattern: Too frequent evaluation with small eval_steps causes timeouts. eval_steps=500 with 67k samples = ~134 evaluations per epoch, severely slowing training.
learnings.context: When training on large datasets with small batch_size=1, eval_steps must be much larger (5000+) to avoid evaluation overhead.

learnings.pattern: batch_size=1 is too slow for practical fine-tuning. Use larger batch sizes (8-16) to reduce per-epoch time.
learnings.context: When fine-tuning on 67k+ samples, batch_size=1 makes training prohibitively slow.

learnings.pattern: Starting training without verifying evaluation data availability causes incomplete benchmarks.
learnings.context: Always verify validation labels exist before starting training runs to ensure metrics can be captured.

learnings.pattern: LoRA PEFT implementation for DistilGPT-2 works correctly with HF Trainer and target_modules=['c_attn', 'c_proj'].
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA on DistilGPT-2, targeting attention weights (c_attn, c_proj) is the correct approach.

learnings.pattern: MPS (Metal Performance Shaders) on Apple Silicon has runtime incompatibilities with HuggingFace Trainer - always have CPU fallback ready
learnings.context: When running training on Apple M-series Macs, the MPS backend may fail mid-training requiring restart with CPU

learnings.pattern: LoRA PEFT enables parameter-efficient fine-tuning with only ~2.8% of trainable params (1.2M vs 44M), making it viable for limited GPU memory
learnings.context: When comparing LoRA vs full fine-tuning on DistilGPT-2 with 44M parameters

learnings.pattern: LoRA hyperparameters r=16, alpha=32, dropout=0.1 combined with learning rate 3e-4 and 3 epochs provided stable convergence on SST-2
learnings.context: When fine-tuning DistilGPT-2 on sentiment classification with the PEFT library

learnings.pattern: Small dataset sizes (1,000 samples from SST-2) lead to high variance (±3.35% std) making statistical comparison between methods unreliable
learnings.context: When benchmarking LoRA vs full fine-tuning with constrained dataset sizes

learnings.pattern: HuggingFace PEFT library provides well-tested LoRA implementation with native Trainer integration, eliminating need for custom LoRA logic
learnings.context: When implementing parameter-efficient fine-tuning on HuggingFace models

learnings.pattern: The LoRA fine-tuning script creation failed twice, indicating insufficient initial planning before coding. Both attempts resulted in scripts with issues causing hangs.
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA fine-tuning scripts for small language models

learnings.pattern: Scripts without robust error handling and debugging mechanisms can hang indefinitely, making it difficult to diagnose failures during fine-tuning.
learnings.context: When running LoRA fine-tuning experiments that may encounter runtime issues

learnings.pattern: PEFT library is the appropriate tool for implementing LoRA fine-tuning on DistilGPT-2, providing configurable rank r parameter support.
learnings.context: When setting up LoRA experiments for parameter-efficient fine-tuning

learnings.pattern: Starting with rank r=8 is a reasonable baseline for LoRA experiments on small language models like DistilGPT-2, allowing for meaningful rank comparison studies.
learnings.context: When selecting LoRA rank hyperparameters for small model fine-tuning

learnings.pattern: SST-2 dataset uses 'dev' split, not 'validation'. Always check available splits with dataset['train'].unique('split') or dataset.keys() before accessing data.
learnings.context: When loading HuggingFace datasets for evaluation, verify split names exist by printing dataset.keys() or checking dataset['train'].unique('split')

learnings.pattern: Use AutoTokenizer and AutoModelForCausalLM instead of specific model tokenizers (DistilGPT2Tokenizer) to avoid import errors.
learnings.context: When loading pretrained models for LoRA fine-tuning

learnings.pattern: Writing results only at script end causes data loss on truncation. Add immediate file writing after each evaluation epoch or checkpoint.
learnings.context: When running long training experiments that may produce truncated output

learnings.pattern: Incomplete function modifications (e.g., changing dataset split but not updating all code paths) cause NoneType errors during Trainer initialization.
learnings.context: When refactoring code - ensure all dependent code paths are updated together

learnings.pattern: HuggingFace Trainer + PEFT LoRA frequently fails on Apple MPS backend with NoneType errors. Always fallback to CPU immediately when MPS fails.
learnings.context: When using PEFT/LoRA with HuggingFace Trainer on Apple Silicon

learnings.pattern: Explorer agents cannot execute code - they can only search and retrieve information, making them unsuitable for tasks requiring validation through execution.
learnings.context: When using agents for research that requires verifying code functionality or running experiments

learnings.pattern: MPS device detection works during model loading but doesn't automatically enforce CPU at training runtime - explicit device enforcement in training loop is required.
learnings.context: When running on Apple Silicon with MPS backend where automatic fallback detection exists but training may still attempt MPS

learnings.pattern: rsLoRA scaling (scaling = learning_rate * sqrt((r + rs_factor * r) / r)) must be explicitly applied for ranks >= 16 to maintain training stability.
learnings.context: When configuring LoRA with ranks 4 and 16 comparison - rank 16 requires rsLoRA scaling for proper adaptation

learnings.pattern: The deprecated `load_metric` from the datasets library should be replaced with `evaluate.load` to avoid import errors.
learnings.context: When using HuggingFace evaluation metrics in training scripts

learnings.pattern: Creating a centralized config.py with all hyperparameters before writing training code enables modular, reproducible experiments and easier debugging.
learnings.context: When setting up experiments comparing different LoRA ranks or hyperparameter configurations

learnings.pattern: Tokenizer return type inconsistency: when return_tensors=None, tokenizer outputs Python lists, not tensors. Calling .tolist() on lists fails with 'list' object has no attribute 'tolist'
learnings.context: When preparing data for training, tokenizer outputs vary based on return_tensors parameter

learnings.pattern: Use GPT2Tokenizer instead of non-existent DistilGPT2Tokenizer class from transformers
learnings.context: When importing tokenizer for DistilGPT-2 model

learnings.pattern: Evaluation scripts may trigger 'fabrication detection' errors if they don't properly load and process existing data files
learnings.context: When writing evaluation scripts that reference data paths created by preparation scripts

learnings.pattern: Read existing files before creating new ones to understand data formats and avoid mismatches
learnings.context: Before writing training or evaluation scripts, inspect data preparation outputs

learnings.pattern: Explorer tasks may fail or produce incomplete output if queries are too broad or cut off mid-execution
learnings.context: When searching for best practices or papers using explorer tool

learnings.pattern: DistilGPT2Tokenizer does not exist in HuggingFace - must use GPT2Tokenizer instead
learnings.context: When loading tokenizers for DistilGPT-2 model, the dedicated DistilGPT2Tokenizer class is not available

learnings.pattern: PEFT + HuggingFace Trainer integration on CPU requires setup BEFORE torch imports to avoid compatibility failures
learnings.context: When integrating PEFT LoraConfig with HF Trainer, CPU fallback configuration must happen before any torch imports

learnings.pattern: Multiple rewrite attempts failed because files were not being saved - need to verify file persistence after each change
learnings.context: When fixing integration issues, rewriting the script multiple times without verifying save success led to repeated failures

learnings.pattern: Data collator and padding token handling are critical integration points that cause PEFT+Trainer failures if misconfigured
learnings.context: When setting up LoRA fine-tuning with Trainer, the data collator must properly handle padding tokens

learnings.pattern: Using PEFT library abstracts away most LoRA implementation details - only LoraConfig with target_modules needed
learnings.context: When configuring LoRA for small language models like DistilGPT-2, PEFT provides clean abstractions for rank and target module selection

learnings.pattern: Starting with full SST-2 dataset and multiple epochs causes timeout on CPU with 10-minute budget. Must reduce to 1000 samples and 1 epoch to complete training.
learnings.context: When training on CPU with limited time budget (10 min) and memory (4GB)

learnings.pattern: PEFT library provides reliable LoRA configuration for DistilGPT-2 with configurable rank, alpha, and dropout parameters.
learnings.context: When setting up LoRA fine-tuning on small transformer models like DistilGPT-2

learnings.pattern: HuggingFace datasets library successfully loads and tokenizes SST-2 for sentiment classification tasks.
learnings.context: When loading standard NLP datasets like SST-2 for text classification

learnings.pattern: Initial training script implementation failed - device handling and import issues caused failures before timeout.
learnings.context: When writing training scripts that import from multiple custom modules (load_sst2.py, setup_lora.py)

learnings.pattern: For LoRA on DistilGPT-2 sentiment classification, rank 4 vs rank 16 comparison requires keeping other hyperparameters (alpha=32, dropout=0.1) constant while varying only rank.
learnings.context: When conducting LoRA rank ablation studies on text classification

learnings.pattern: CRITICAL: Use 'eval_strategy' NOT 'evaluation_strategy' in TrainingArguments — deprecated in transformers >=4.46, removed in 4.50+. Will crash with TypeError. Same applies to save_strategy, logging_strategy.
learnings.context: Any HuggingFace Trainer script — always use eval_strategy instead of evaluation_strategy

learnings.pattern: Using only 500 samples for rank=16 while using 1600 samples for rank=4 created an unfair comparison - results were not comparable due to dataset size mismatch.
learnings.context: When comparing LoRA ranks, ensure both use identical dataset sizes

learnings.pattern: 500-sample subsets are too small for stable sentiment classification experiments - high variance in results across seeds.
learnings.context: When running quick experiments, balance sample size against time constraints but rerun with larger data for valid conclusions

learnings.pattern: PEFT library with AutoModelForCausalLM works well for LoRA fine-tuning on GPT-2 models, avoiding need to manually configure LoRA layers.
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA on causal language models like DistilGPT-2

learnings.pattern: Using DistilBERT tokenizer with GPT-2 model causes mismatch errors - must use GPT2Tokenizer for GPT-based models.
learnings.context: When preparing tokenizers for GPT-family models

learnings.pattern: Column name mismatch between HuggingFace datasets and local JSON files caused evaluation failures. SST-2 uses 'sentence' but local files used 'text'.
learnings.context: When converting datasets to local format and loading from different sources (HuggingFace vs local JSON), column names may differ.

learnings.pattern: Evaluation script failed to load checkpoints multiple times, blocking metric extraction. The full pipeline was not validated end-to-end before execution.
learnings.context: When running rank comparison experiments without testing evaluation pipeline in advance.

learnings.pattern: PEFT library AutoModelForSequenceClassification had issues with DistilGPT-2. Consider using direct transformers library with LoRA implementation or verify PEFT compatibility first.
learnings.context: When setting up LoRA fine-tuning on smaller GPT models like DistilGPT-2.

learnings.pattern: Training script execution failed due to environment issues (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES configuration). Ensure runtime environment is properly configured before running experiments.
learnings.context: When executing training scripts in constrained environments.

learnings.pattern: Explorer successfully found multiple relevant papers on LoRA for sentiment analysis including original LoRA paper, LOFO, and sentiment-specific implementations.
learnings.context: Literature search tasks completed successfully with comprehensive paper coverage.

learnings.pattern: Always validate data integrity immediately after loading - check for invalid labels, missing values, and proper data types before training
learnings.context: When preparing MRPC dataset, labels were all -1 indicating loading/processing errors that weren't caught until training failed

learnings.pattern: Implement checkpoint saving with periodic frequency and timeout handling for long training runs to prevent total loss on interruption
learnings.context: BERT teacher training required multiple restarts; adding checkpointing and timeout handling was critical for completion

learnings.pattern: Benchmark scripts for inference speed need aggressive optimization (smaller batch sizes, fewer samples, warmup runs) to complete within timeout limits
learnings.context: Measuring inference speed failed repeatedly due to timeouts - needed a faster version with reduced sample sizes

learnings.pattern: Use multiple random seeds (e.g., 42, 123, 456) and report mean/std performance to get reliable accuracy comparisons between teacher, baseline, and distilled student
learnings.context: When comparing knowledge distillation effectiveness, single-run results can be misleading due to variance

learnings.pattern: Incomplete experiment pipeline: tasks were initiated but not all executed to completion, leaving no results for evaluation
learnings.context: When running multi-stage research experiments requiring sequential task completion

learnings.pattern: Training script was incomplete - dataset loading was implemented but actual training loop was missing
learnings.context: When writing training scripts; always verify full pipeline functionality before attempting experiments

learnings.pattern: CPU-only training is prohibitively slow for transformer fine-tuning; use GPU or significantly reduce dataset size (e.g., 2000 samples)
learnings.context: When fine-tuning DistilGPT-2 or similar models on CPU

learnings.pattern: PEFT library provides clean abstractions for LoRA implementation on HuggingFace models
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA fine-tuning on GPT-style models

learnings.pattern: Import errors in training scripts often stem from missing utility functions. Define helper functions like set_seed in shared utility modules before importing them.
learnings.context: When setting up LoRA training scripts with PEFT, ensure all imported functions are actually defined in the source modules

learnings.pattern: Reviewer tasks that depend on training run results cannot execute before training completes. The result JSON files need to exist before loading them.
learnings.context: When planning experiment workflows, ensure data generation tasks (training) complete before analysis tasks that depend on that data

learnings.pattern: PEFT library provides clean abstractions for LoRA fine-tuning on GPT-2/DistilGPT-2 with minimal code changes from base model training.
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA on transformer models for classification, PEFT simplifies the setup significantly

learnings.pattern: Using HuggingFace datasets library for SST-2 provides a standardized, pre-processed format that integrates seamlessly with training pipelines.
learnings.context: When preparing sentiment classification datasets, the datasets library offers reliable data loading

learnings.pattern: Comparing LoRA ranks (4 vs 16) with multiple seeds (42, 123) enables statistical robustness in accuracy comparisons.
learnings.context: When evaluating LoRA rank impact on model performance, using multiple random seeds reduces variance in results

learnings.pattern: Using a smaller dataset subset (2000 samples) enabled feasible CPU training while still achieving meaningful accuracy comparisons
learnings.context: When training LoRA on DistilGPT-2 with limited compute resources, subset sampling is necessary

learnings.pattern: Full SST-2 dataset (67k samples) on CPU was prohibitively slow, requiring dataset reduction
learnings.context: When fine-tuning GPT-2 family models on CPU without GPU acceleration

learnings.pattern: rank=4 and rank=16 achieved nearly identical accuracy (~81.5%), suggesting lower rank may be sufficient for sentiment classification
learnings.context: When comparing LoRA rank hyperparameters for downstream sentiment tasks

learnings.pattern: Multiple random seeds (seed42, seed123) are needed for reliable accuracy comparisons across configurations
learnings.context: When comparing hyperparameter settings to account for variance in fine-tuning results

learnings.pattern: Using PEFT library with HuggingFace transformers provides clean LoRA implementation for GPT-2 family models
learnings.context: When implementing parameter-efficient fine-tuning on GPT-2 variants

learnings.pattern: Using only 2000 samples for quick validation resulted in incomplete experiments - the rank=4 training was truncated and rank=16 failed entirely, preventing valid comparison
learnings.context: When running ablation studies comparing LoRA ranks, insufficient dataset size or training time leads to unreliable/no results

learnings.pattern: Training scripts need checkpointing and result persistence - when experiments fail or get interrupted, no results are saved for analysis
learnings.context: When running long training jobs that may timeout or crash, implement periodic saving and explicit result output before completion

learnings.pattern: The AutoModelForSequenceClassification with peft library successfully loads DistilGPT-2 with LoRA config, indicating this is the correct API approach
learnings.context: When implementing LoRA fine-tuning on GPT-based models for classification

learnings.pattern: Higher LoRA ranks (r=16) may require more training time or different hyperparameters than lower ranks (r=4) - cannot simply swap and expect same behavior
learnings.context: When comparing LoRA rank ablation studies, each rank may need tuning

learnings.pattern: Explorer tasks successfully found best practices and papers on LoRA fine-tuning - web search is reliable for gathering background research
learnings.context: When starting new research missions, the explorer component reliably provides foundational knowledge

learnings.pattern: LoRA rank=4 achieves nearly identical accuracy (83.7%) to rank=16 (83.83%) on SST-2, suggesting lower rank is sufficient for this task
learnings.context: When fine-tuning DistilGPT-2 on SST-2 sentiment classification, increasing rank from 4 to 16 provides marginal accuracy gain (~0.1%)

learnings.pattern: Using a small subset (first 2000 samples) for initial verification enables fast iteration and debugging before full training
learnings.context: When testing new training scripts or hyperparameters, verify on a subset first to catch errors quickly

learnings.pattern: Training runs may fail silently or produce errors; always re-run with proper arguments and verify output files are created
learnings.context: When running experiments with different seeds/parameters, check that all result files are properly saved

learnings.pattern: PEFT library provides clean abstractions for LoRA implementation, reducing boilerplate and errors
learnings.context: When implementing parameter-efficient fine-tuning, using established libraries like PEFT ensures reliable results

learnings.pattern: Multiple seeds are necessary for robust comparison; single-seed results may be misleading due to variance
learnings.context: When comparing hyperparameters like LoRA rank, test with at least 2-3 seeds to account for random variation
